title: Presentation Zen: Simple Ideas on Presentation Design and Delivery
subtitle: A Guide to Breaking Free from Bullet-Point Culture and Communicating with Clarity
author: Garr Reynolds
themes: White space (ma) as a primary design element
Audience transformation as the measure of success
Authenticity over technique in live delivery
Breaking the tyranny of default PowerPoint templates
The Rule of Three as a cognitive constraint
excerpt: Garr Reynolds shows why most presentations fail not because of poor delivery but because of poor design rooted in poor thinking — and offers a Zen-infused framework for creating talks that connect, persuade, and endure through simplicity, visual thinking, and mindful preparation.
